description: Chunked bulk API: POST up to 50 IDs per request with one POST per chunk (executeOnce on the HTTP node within each batch when using Loop Over Items). Code-based chunking that emits ≤50 IDs per downstream item is also valid.

conversation.text: Daily at midnight, fetch all pending user IDs from GET https://api.example.com/users/pending-update and push them to POST https://api.example.com/users/bulk-update. The endpoint accepts at most 50 IDs per request — if there are more than 50, send them in chunks.

outcomeExpectations: IDs are chunked so no single bulk-update request carries more than 50 IDs (Loop Over Items with batch size 50, or an equivalent chunking step such as Code).
Each bulk-update HTTP request sends one POST per chunk carrying multiple IDs (executeOnce on the HTTP node when inside a batch), not one POST per individual ID.
The workflow results in exactly two bulk POSTs for 55 IDs (50 + 5), not 55 separate single-ID POSTs.

executionScenarios.name: fifty-five-ids-two-requests
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. POST https://api.example.com/users/bulk-update is called exactly twice — first with 50 IDs, second with 5 IDs — not 55 separate POSTs with one ID each. All 55 IDs are included across the two requests.
